post_fix_evidence.result: 6 passed — all RED tests (previously 5 failed) now pass after model_dump(mode='json') fix

class_e_intent_alignment.audit_record_summary: The audit record at L181 documents that validate_document() at promptverge/schemas/validator.py:22 calls model_dump() which returns native Python objects (uuid.UUID, datetime, date). Line 23 passes this dict to jsonschema.validate() against model_json_schema(), which declares doc_id as {type: 'string', format: 'uuid'}. jsonschema's isinstance(value, str) check fails for uuid.UUID instances, raising ValidationError, so the function returns False. Every real PromptVerge document (CodeAudit, ProductRequirementsDocument, DeepWorkTask, KnowledgeGraphQuiz) carries doc_id: uuid.UUID, making validate_document uniformly broken for all of them. The audit record identifies the fix as replacing model_dump() with model_dump(mode='json').
class_e_intent_alignment.alignment_assessment: This change directly addresses the documented defect at its root cause: line 22 of validator.py now reads model_dump(mode='json'), which coerces uuid.UUID → str, datetime → ISO-8601 str, date → ISO-8601 str before passing the dict to jsonschema. The two new tests in test_completeness.py fulfill the GOAL signal: test_validator_success_real_document confirms validate_document(CodeAudit(...real instance...)) returns True (previously False), and test_validator_failure_tampered_real_document confirms a tampered instance (invalid source_commit_sha bypassing Pydantic validation via model_construct) returns False via the real jsonschema path without mocking.
